description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with an olive green fuselage and a compact, aerodynamic body. It features a four-blade main rotor and a ducted tail rotor, both in black, providing stability and maneuverability. The cockpit has a large, dark-tinted front window and smaller side windows, offering wide visibility for the crew. Mounted beneath the fuselage are four external hardpoints, each carrying a cylindrical payload, likely representing missiles or fuel tanks. The helicopter rests on two sturdy black landing skids with crossbars for support. The overall design suggests a light attack or reconnaissance role, suitable for urban operations.
description: Olive green military helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, ducted tail rotor, and external payloads mounted beneath the fuselage. Features a large tinted cockpit window and black landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, tinted cockpit window, the four-blade main rotor, and the symmetrical landing skids.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the external payloads, streamlined fuselage, and the ducted tail rotor at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, ducted tail rotor, and the alignment of the external payloads beneath the fuselage.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the external payloads, cockpit profile, and landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades, black in color, with no visible markings. The blades appear to be made of composite material and are evenly spaced around the central hub.
attribute_tail_rotor: Ducted fan-style tail rotor with multiple short blades enclosed in a circular shroud, positioned horizontally at the end of the tail boom. The rotor and shroud are black.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel black landing skids made of metal or composite, connected by crossbars for stability. The skids are straight with slight upward curves at the ends.
attribute_cockpit_windows: One large, curved front cockpit window and two smaller side windows, all dark-tinted with minimal visible framing. The windows provide wide visibility for the crew.
